judgment.summary: Extremely high growth company with excellent margins and strong cash flow, trading at a very low forward P/E despite a high trailing P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 59.7%; next earnings date 2026-04-30.
positives: Revenue growth 251.0% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Operating margin 70.0% and Profit margin 34.2% — industry-leading profitability.
Return on equity 39.3% and Return on assets 22.8% — highly efficient capital deployment.
Forward P/E 8.76x vs trailing 52.52x — suggests significant expected earnings growth.
Free cash flow $2.26B TTM — strong cash generation to fund operations and growth.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio 1.50 — leverage is somewhat elevated.
Price/Sales 17.25x and Price/Book 22.25x — valuations are high on these metrics.
Analyst mean target $1493.36 is below the current spot price of $1534.44.
Jim Cramer commentary suggests a bearish short-term outlook for the stock.

news.paragraph: Despite Sandisk's (SNDK) strong earnings beat last quarter, the stock is facing investor skepticism, with Jim Cramer suggesting it needs to "come down more." This sentiment follows Nvidia's earnings, which, despite being impressive, also failed to fully satisfy investors, impacting related stocks like Seagate and Western Digital. The broader narrative around AI's influence on dividends and the valuation of tech stocks is also a factor traders should consider.

headlines.title: Nvidia's Impressive Earnings Fall Flat with Investors. Has the Stock Lost Its Edge?
Blame AI for the Strange Death of Dividends
Seagate Stock, Western Digital Bounce Back After Nvidia Results
Jim Cramer on Sandisk: “Gotta Let It Come Down More”
Jim Cramer on Western Digital: “That Seems Too Rich to Me”

examples.body: $MU $SNDK Why is SNDK zooming and this Micron POS is dragging?
$SNDK Need an another ATH plus some so we can raise our stops. Thought we were going to get tapped at 1200.
